step.title: Generate i18n translator-foundation goal (single goal, phase=cross-cutting)
step.rule: Scaffold goal (phase=scaffold) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs — the translator foundation depends only on the repo skeleton.

This step is UNCONDITIONAL — the php-symfony pack carries PHP-I18N-001 as a `must` code-rule, so every greenfield plan establishes a single-locale translator foundation even while ADR-013 defers full i18n. Never split.

Single-locale only (English per ADR-013): this goal establishes the translator config + a TranslatableDomainException base so user-facing strings/backend errors are translatable. It does NOT add a second locale, locale negotiation, or per-string extraction — that is the implementer's downstream concern under PHP-I18N-001.
depends_on = [SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID] ONLY — never the domain/error-handling goals (no rewiring; scope guard).

substep.title: Collect scaffold goal ID
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter for phase=scaffold. Store the goal ID as SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID (goal-002 in a fresh plan).

check.action: ABORT with error: "No scaffold goal (phase=scaffold) found in goals.yaml — cannot generate translator-foundation goal without the repo skeleton"

substep.rule: SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID MUST be dynamically resolved from goals.yaml — never hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Resolve shared-kernel deliverables from layout.md
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/layout.md `## Layers` (written by discover / created by scaffold substep 2.2b). Bind SRC_ROOTS from the `- Source roots:` line. The translator foundation lives in the shared-kernel context contexts/previo (the published language every other context depends on), so its translatable-exception base is reusable repo-wide and its *.php path matches the PHP-I18N-001 `{src}/**/*.php` glob.

Bind DELIVERABLES = contexts/previo/src/Exception/TranslatableDomainException.php (translatable exception base carrying a message key, NOT sprintf'd English), config/packages/translation.yaml (single default locale = en, fallback = en), translations/ (the catalogue directory, e.g. translations/messages.en.yaml).

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (I18N-01)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with the translator-foundation criterion:

criteria.item: PHP-I18N-001 — translator configured (single English locale per ADR-013, fallback en) and a TranslatableDomainException base exists carrying a message key; user-facing strings/backend errors resolve through the translation catalogue, not sprintf'd English

substep.rule: I18N-01 is UNCONDITIONAL and explicitly names PHP-I18N-001 + translator — it is the GENUINE adoption surface for the `must` i18n rule (the cross-cutting shards 3.22/3.24/3.25/3.28 only run the injection pass for mechanical parity; their phase tokens are not rule phases).

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with bare commands proving the translator foundation exists:
substep.rule: Commands are emitted BARE (CMD-CONV): the daemon wraps them into the app container in docker mode. TranslatableDomainException presence + translator config prove the foundation; full per-string adoption is the rule's downstream concern.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Translator-foundation goal (phase=cross-cutting): establish the single-locale Symfony translator (default + fallback locale = en per ADR-013) and a TranslatableDomainException base in the contexts/previo shared kernel so user-facing strings and backend errors are translatable from the start. This is the GENUINE adoption surface for php-symfony code-rule PHP-I18N-001 (globs {src}/**/*.php + **/templates/**/*.twig). ADR-013 defers full multi-locale i18n, but the must-rule requires the translatable boundary to exist — this goal provides it without forcing a second locale. Depends on the scaffold goal: {{SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID}} — needs the bootable skeleton + contexts/previo shared kernel.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — translator config + TranslatableDomainException base + catalogue stub are tightly coupled and small.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Translator config
investigator.pass: translation.yaml present with a single default/fallback locale (en); the translator service resolves the catalogue
investigator.fail: Missing translation.yaml, no configured locale, or translator service unregistered

investigator.name: Translatable exception base
investigator.pass: TranslatableDomainException base exists in the shared kernel, carrying a message key (not a sprintf'd English string)
investigator.fail: No translatable exception base, or it concatenates/sprintf's user-facing English instead of carrying a message key

section.rule: Exactly 2 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09). The convention-audit investigator may be MERGED/extended by [CODE-RULE-INJECTION] (substep 3.21b.6b) when the cross-cutting match returns review rules.

section.name: not-in-scope
section.value: A second locale, locale negotiation/switching, full per-string extraction across existing controllers/templates, domain/error-handling exception rewiring — the foundation establishes the translator + translatable base only; per-call-site adoption is the implementer's downstream concern under PHP-I18N-001.

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined translator-foundation deliverable paths (contexts/previo/src/Exception/TranslatableDomainException.php + config/packages/translation.yaml + translations/). Bind RULE_PHASE = `cross-cutting` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM; do NOT remap it).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (3.21b.3); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (3.21b.4); fold `review` `validate_cmd`s into the existing convention-audit investigator (MERGE — append to its Commands; base is 2 from 3.21b.5, so the count stays legal); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (3.21b.5). Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.
substep.rule: `cross-cutting` IS a real Go rule phase: PHP-I18N-001 (phase=cross-cutting; globs {src}/**/*.php + **/templates/**/*.twig) matches the contexts/previo/src/Exception/*.php deliverable, so its `CR-PHP-I18N-001` acceptance_line injects — GENUINE enforcement, NOT mechanical parity. PRESERVE the 2 existing investigators (the convention-audit one is MERGED, never duplicated).

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP and record ID
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Establish single-locale translator foundation + TranslatableDomainException base (PHP-I18N-001)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.21b.3 (I18N-01) plus any CR-PHP-I18N-001 acceptance_line injected by substep 3.21b.6b
VALIDATE array from substep 3.21b.4 (3 bare commands) plus any injected validate_cmd
CONTEXT string from substep 3.21b.5 (purpose + fan-out hint + not-in-scope + injected Code rules line)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.21b.5's 2 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table), as MERGED by substep 3.21b.6b.
["contexts/previo/src/Exception/**", "config/packages/translation.yaml", "translations/**"] — the translator-foundation footprint (file-disjoint from domain/error-handling goals).
[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID] — the scaffold goal ID collected in substep 3.21b.1 (NEVER the domain/error-handling goals)

substep.rule: Description is 94 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — a focused single-locale foundation, fixable in few cycles.

phase=cross-cutting on the created goal — a REAL Go rule phase, so PHP-I18N-001 genuinely injects (the whole point of this goal). It shares PHASE_ORDER rank=10 with the other cross-cutting phases (added in substep 4.2), so it orders legally.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Record the returned goal ID as I18N_FOUNDATION_GOAL_ID.
Print: "Translator foundation created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=cross-cutting, depends_on=[{{SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID}}]) with 2 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
